location_dictionary.notes: СС = Saltivka/Severnaya Saltivka ambiguous; most often Saltivka proper
СС highly ambiguous — may mean Saltivka or Severnaya Saltivka depending on context
Industrial district, southeastern Kharkiv
НД likely = Немышля/Докучаєвське area; verify context
Also a village near Chuhuiv oblast — ambiguous by context
ЛГ likely = Leninska hora / Leninsky district area
ПП = Ploscha Povstannia (metro/landmark), central Kharkiv
Refers to Kharkiv International Airport (Osnova area)
Likely refers to Rzhevka marshalling yard / Rogan area — confirm with local knowledge
Likely refers to Horizon district or industrial landmark near ХТЗ area
НД ambiguous — also could be Nemyshlya/Dokuchayevske combined
Could be Kharkiv suburb or separate village — verify
Kharkiv aviation plant area / landmark
Pitomnyk — railway/settlement node north of Kharkiv
Ambiguous: also exists as Kharkiv district name
МД = Мала Данилівка — frequently used abbreviation
East of Zolochiv; FPV/drone strikes noted here
Бурулк = typo of Бурлук
Separate from Malynivka (Kharkiv district) — context needed
КуНР = likely 'Курська НаРодна' (Kursk region) — launch origin direction
З = Західний (Western), Б = Більшовик or specific village name
Ambiguous — could be Lozova city or Ruska/Chorna Lozova settlements
Солобожанское = typo
Sarcastic/shorthand for Russia/Belgorod Oblast; used to mark origin direction
Derogatory nickname for MiG-31 friendly interceptor aircraft

weapon_dictionary.label: Shahed/Geran drone
weapon_dictionary.notes: Шаболда = most common slang for Shahed-136/131. 'Реактивна шаболда' = faster variant (Shahed-238 or similar); 'Р шаболда' = reactive/fast shahed

weapon_dictionary.label: Unidentified UAV
weapon_dictionary.notes: БПЛП = typo of БПЛА; Ударный = strike UAV (sub-type of bpla)

weapon_dictionary.label: FPV drone
weapon_dictionary.notes: First-person-view attack drone; small, fast, high maneuverability

weapon_dictionary.label: Molniya UAV (reconnaissance/strike)
weapon_dictionary.notes: Molniya = RU-produced attack/recon drone. In messages often starts as 'unknown' then updated to 'молния'. Also name of RU drone factory in Taganrog.

weapon_dictionary.label: Reconnaissance UAV
weapon_dictionary.notes: Развед. = reconnaissance. Суперкам = SuperCam fixed-wing recon drone. Zala/Зала = ZALA Aero group recon UAV

weapon_dictionary.label: Lancet loitering munition
weapon_dictionary.notes: Lancet = RU Lancet-3 loitering munition; typically paired with Zala recon

weapon_dictionary.label: V2U group drone swarm
weapon_dictionary.notes: V2U = likely operational designation for specific drone type or formation used in this channel; possibly Shahed sub-variant or local tracking code

weapon_dictionary.label: KAB glide bomb
weapon_dictionary.notes: KAB-500/1500 — Russian guided aerial bombs; launched by Su-34/Su-35. Петухи = su-34/35 aircraft

weapon_dictionary.label: MLRS (РСЗО)
weapon_dictionary.notes: Multiple-launch rocket system; Grad, Uragan, Tornado. Often threat to northern suburbs/oblast.

weapon_dictionary.label: Cruise missile (KR)
weapon_dictionary.notes: КР = крилата ракета (cruise missile). Separate from ballistic.

weapon_dictionary.label: Ballistic missile
weapon_dictionary.notes: Ballistic threat — Iskander, S-300 repurposed, etc. Launch markers include Taganrog, Stary Oskol, Rostov

weapon_dictionary.label: S-300 (repurposed surface-to-surface)
weapon_dictionary.notes: Not directly named in messages; ballistic alerts from Taganrog/Rostov often correspond to S-300 use

weapon_dictionary.label: Петухи (Russian strike aircraft — Su-34/35)
weapon_dictionary.notes: Slang for RU strike aircraft; 'петухи заходят на пуски' = aircraft making attack run for KAB release

weapon_dictionary.label: Гербера (unidentified/special drone type)
weapon_dictionary.notes: Appears once: 'это гербера, локационно не фиксируется' — possibly a stealth or low-observable drone; rare; unconfirmed type

weapon_dictionary.label: Перехватчик (friendly interceptor)
weapon_dictionary.notes: Friendly assets — interceptor aircraft or UA drones; 'добрые' = friendly/own

phrase_patterns.description: Unit passing through or moving via a waypoint
examples: Через Циркуны группа(4-7 шт.) V2U на СС

phrase_patterns.description: Repeated/ongoing threat or same target again
examples: Заходят на повторные пуски по Боровой
Снова взяла курс на Ч Лозовую

phrase_patterns.description: Correction or type update to previous report
examples: Это БПЛА V2U курс на СС

phrase_patterns.description: Area cleared / threat passed / all clear
examples: На сейчас в области и над городом чисто 🫡
Вылетело из города на Ч. Лозовую/Дергачи

На сейчас:
1 Шевченково
3 с БНР курс на Золочев

phrase_patterns.description: Incoming launch / aircraft making attack run
examples: Петухи заходят на пуски по северной части области
Угроза РСЗО для северной части области/пригорода
Запуски РСЗО по северной части области

top_ambiguous_terms.resolution: Context clue: if paired with БД → Saltivka district. If alone → likely Severnaya Saltivka
Often paired with СС/Жуки — likely refers to a northeastern Kharkiv zone along Belhorodska road. Needs geo confirmation.
High confidence it = Мала Данилівка (settlement north of Kharkiv). Confirm with map.
Most likely Ploshcha Povstannia — central Kharkiv metro/square area. Geo confirm.
Context: pairs with Немышля → Nemyshlya/Dokuchayevske corridor. Needs map anchor.
Most likely Kholodna Hora district. Confirm with map.
Likely Leninska Hora (Lenin Hill area, west Kharkiv). Confirm.
Paired with ХТЗ — likely Rzhevsky microrayon east of ХТЗ. Needs geo anchor.
Often appears as type update. Initially 'unknown' then 'молния'. Confirmed RU-produced drone (Molniya). Also has factory named Atlant-Aero in Taganrog.
Disambiguate by trajectory: city-context messages → Kharkiv district; paired with Чугуїв/Есхар → Chuhuiv raion village
Appears as 'БПЛА V2U' in group swarm context. Possibly a local tracker designation for Shahed-type or Orlan. Needs source confirmation.
Context: 'тревога по баллистике с КуНР' → launch from Kursk Oblast. Treat as origin:kursk.
Single occurrence: 'це гербера, локаційно не фіксується' — possibly a low-RCS or fast drone not tracked by station. Needs monitoring.
Consistently used as northern approach waypoint — likely Pitomnyk station area near Derhachi. Confirm geo anchor.
The operator distinguishes 'reactive shahed' explicitly from standard. Likely Shahed-238 jet variant. Treat as shahed subtype with speed flag.

priority_hotspots.notes: Highest-frequency target. Under near-continuous drone harassment (low-altitude 40-60m reported). All fuel stations reportedly destroyed. Major northwest hub.
Second-highest frequency. Key waypoint north/northwest of Kharkiv. KAB strikes and Zala recon regularly mentioned. Multiple FPV strikes noted.
Consistent KAB strike target, repeated salvos. Frontline proximity.
Repeated drone approaches and circling. Key southern Kharkiv landmark and trajectory waypoint.
Northwestern approach corridor node. Very frequent recon and strike drone mention.
West-northwest transit hub. Shahed swarms frequently route through here.
Northern approach corridor. Paired frequently with Bezruky/Derhachi.
Northern ring node. Frequent recon drone presence (Zala).
Northeast Kharkiv entry corridor. V2U swarm groups transit through here en route to city.
Northern border area; KAB and MLRS targets. High escalation zone.
Northern transit node for drones approaching Derhachi/Slaty ne corridor.
